steps.title: Use the parallel-line shortcut
steps.content:
No solution means parallel distinct lines: same slope, different $y$-intercept. Rewrite the given equation:
$$3x=24y-12$$
$$24y=3x+12$$
$$y=\frac{x}{8}+\frac{1}{2}$$
The second line must have slope $\frac{1}{8}$ but a different intercept.

steps.title: Check the matching-slope choice
steps.content: The correct answer gives $$\frac{1}{2}x=4y \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{8}.$$ This has the same slope, $\frac{1}{8}$, and a different intercept, $0$, so the two lines are parallel and distinct.

steps.title: Eliminate the rest
steps.content: A has slope $\frac{1}{4}$, so it intersects the given line. C is exactly the same as the given line, so it has infinitely many solutions. D has slope $\frac{1}{16}$, so it intersects the given line. Therefore, B is correct.
